key_insight: OmniMatBench shows that current multimodal LLMs still struggle with materials-science reasoning beyond simple property prediction, even when problems span many subfields and require expert-level interpretation.
engineering_relevance: The benchmark helps materials engineers identify where AI assistants are reliable, where they fail, and how much human-calibrated reasoning support is needed for research workflows.
method: Built a human-curated benchmark with 3,171 QA and calculation problems
Covers 19 materials-science subfields across fundamental, structural, processing, and functional materials
Evaluated 13 open- and closed-source multimodal LLMs under standard, formula-assisted, retrieval-assisted, and code-assisted settings
evidence_signal: Across 13 evaluated models, the best achieved only 0.372 overall score, indicating a large gap in current multimodal materials reasoning.
watch_item: Performance varies strongly by subfield, so benchmark scores may not translate directly to dependable real-world use in specific materials tasks.
paper_title: OmniMatBench: A Human-Calibrated Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark Across 19 Materials Science Subfields
